behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (three collectors -> aggregation payload), not a single-function algorithm. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this specific omission bug; source clients can be deterministic fakes. MOCK-HIDE: a proper integration test with distinct per-source fixtures will fail if any source is dropped. LLM-DEP: behavior checks input composition before/at summary step, not model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic are rejected because behavior text is about "content passed" rather than real HTTP behavior or summary correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with fake metrics/issue/deploy adapters returning unique sentinel values; assert the exact payload handed to the summary generator contains all three sections and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in cross-module error propagation (source adapter failure -> report assembler fallback text). REAL-INFRA: real network outage is not required to validate fallback policy; deterministic adapter failure reproduces the logic path. MOCK-HIDE: simplistic mocks could hide issues, but a realistic failing adapter fake (exception/timeout result) exposes them. LLM-DEP: requirement is fallback behavior, not model judgment quality. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior does not require proving real transport-layer outage handling, only graceful degradation semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with two successful source adapters and one adapter that fails; verify output report is still generated and includes a `data unavailable` marker in the failed section while other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the real summary model generates from input values; stubbed outputs would always pass and cannot detect factual drift/hallucination. MOCK-HIDE: mocking summary generation hides the exact failure this behavior targets (misrepresentation of key values). MULTI-STEP: only one model decision step is under test, so Workflow is not required. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified by behavior text "accurately represents" which is output-quality/model-judgment dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model on representative weekly datasets with known key figures; score whether produced narrative preserves critical values/facts within acceptance criteria across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this explicitly requires real external boundaries (source APIs + email transport + inbox delivery), which in-process fakes cannot fully reproduce. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email/API clients can pass while real auth, TLS, routing, provider responses, or mailbox delivery semantics fail. LLM-DEP: success criterion here is end-to-end delivery to inbox, not evaluation of model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text focuses on delivery path ("reaches ... inbox correctly") rather than model decision correctness; Workflow rejected because no multi-agent decision pipeline is asserted.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled pipeline in an environment using real endpoints (or production-like live services) and a real recipient mailbox; verify the email is delivered to inbox with the generated report content and expected metadata.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in orchestration/state handoff (send failure -> local persistence -> next-run retry). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced without real external email outage by using a failing delivery adapter and local persistence; no subprocess/OS primitive beyond normal app I/O is strictly required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real network/subprocess for this behavior, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM-stage handoff semantics. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require validating provider-specific network behavior, only fallback-and-retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two-run integration scenario: first run uses failing email adapter and asserts report is persisted locally; second run simulates scheduler-triggered next run with recovered adapter and asserts persisted report is retried and sent, then marked/cleared appropriately.
